title: Global #macros in projects
author: vrad-exe
body:
### Which component should be improved?

Compiler

### Describe your feature suggestion in more detail

I noticed that UMT's compiler supports the GMS2 `#macro` syntax, but macros only seem to be usable within the scope of the code entry where they're defined which limits their usefulness, as opposed to actual GMS2 where they are global. It would be nice if when using the project system, defined macros would be available in all code entries which are being saved into the project.

title: Debug.csx crashing on newest Deltarune Chapter 5 version
author: JadeJuno
body: ### Describe the bug When trying to run the UTDR Script `Debug.csx` on Deltarune Chapter 5 version 0.0.247, it doesn't work, instead showing the following error popup: This error did not occur on Deltarune Chapter 5 version 0.0.243. Checking the error message and the code for the `Debug.csx` script, it seems to be caused by [this line](https://github.com/UnderminersTeam/UndertaleModTool/blob/3faad3b8f33ffad03eab1baf8cb892e90f3aa9db/UndertaleModTool/Scripts/UTDR%20Scripts/Debug.csx#L139) specifically added by commit c3dbb2b8ba2c5cde7fc185e4bc3db9e742dd0728. Seems like whatever issue occured when running the script on Chapter 5 before 0.0.247 was resolved on this version, so that line is unneeded. ### Reproducing steps 1. Open Deltarune Chapter 5's version 0.0.247 `data.win` 2. Try running `Debug.csx` (Scripts > UTDR Scripts > Debug.csx) 3. See error message ### Setup Details UndertaleModTool version: 0.9.1.1 Operating System: Windows 10 GameMaker Game: [DELTARUNE](https://store.steampowered.com/app/1671210/DELTARUNE/) (Chapter 5)

title: UMT automatically removing the "$" accessor from my code.
author: wand-am
body:
### Describe the bug

im working on a translation mod for the first chapter of deltarune and i wrote a small function which takes in a string and declines/conjugates certain verbs and nouns found inside of it. since i have access to the character and enemy names, i figured the most efficient way to find the right words to alter in would be via a struct, such as:
```
struct_darowalx = 
{
    Kris: "darowało",
    Susie: "darowała",
    Ralsei: "darował"
};
```
which associates each character name with a different verb form. from the gml documentation (specifically: https://manual.gamemaker.io/monthly/en/GameMaker_Language/GML_Overview/Accessors.htm#) i understood that it should be possible to access every value inside the struct with the following syntax:
```
struct_darowalx[$ name]
```
where `name` is `global.charname[global.char[caster]]`, a string containing the name of the character doing the action. however, UMT automatically removes the accessor when cleaning up the code, leaving behind `struct_darowalx[name]`. this results in a code error when the function is called, which reads `unable to convert string "Ralsei" to int64` (if ralsei was the one performing the action, with the exact string changing accordingly). im not particularly experienced with gamemaker, but, based on what ive read, i don't think this behavior is intended, and the accessor shouldn't be disappearing from the final code

### Reproducing steps

1. open data.win file
2. write `struct[$ string]` anywhere
3. UMT removes the dollar sign and space alongside all other code cleanup

### Setup Details

1. UMT version: 0.9.1.1
2. OS: endeavourOS (Arch-based Linux distribution)
3. game: DELTARUNE Chapter 1

title: Add the ability to stretch frames
author: Timmy3921
body:
### Which component should be improved?

UI/UX

### Describe your feature suggestion in more detail

Gamemaker lets you stretch frames to allow for variable frame duration within a given animation.

The UTMT sprite editor should have the ability to see and modify stretched frames for certain animations. This can be done by having a text field next to each frame in an animation showing the length of the frame (0 by default).

Not only would this make the sprite editor less cluttered, but stretched frames also have a property where their image_index values are returned as a fraction of one number (i.e. an animation stretched to 4 frames returns, 1.0, 1.25, 1.5, 1.75).

title: Game crashes when using file_text_open_write
author: Rchat42
body: ### Describe the bug I was trying to edit a file using file_text_open_write, but when I save and go to open the game it says that the function "file_open_write" (probably an alias) can't be found. What's even weirder is that the game itself uses that exact same function for its saving functionality. The Functions and Strings tabs in UndertaleModTool also contain the file_open_write entry as well. Here is the specific code used in the KeyPress event: I also tried putting the file editing code inside a function in a global script but I still got the exact same error. In the previous version of UndertaleModTool I was able to use the file_text_open_write function just fine, but now it's not working for some reason. ### Reproducing steps 1) Create a new piece of code 2) Use file_text_open_write in it 3) Assign it as a KeyPress event for some object 4) Save as the new data.win and open the game 5) Game crashes with ERROR in action number 1 of Create Event for object : Error on load Unable to find function file_open_write ### Setup Details On Windows 11 with UndertaleModTool v0.9.0.0 (latest version) for the game "Will You Snail?"

title: Accidentally importing a sprite with missing indexes/frames will lock you out of the Data.win and instantly close when trying to open the sprite.
author: TheBird6053
body: ### Describe the bug Exactly as the title says. I'm workin' on a Five Nights At Frickbear's 3 mod, and when importing some sprites using ImportGraphics.csx I accidentally forgot to rename one of them from sBonnetJumpscareDeathscreen_53 to sBonnetJumpscareDeathscreen_0. From my understanding, that just made it create a 54th frame for the sprite, but only this 54th one. So when opening, it tried to load 53 textures that don't exist, and now it can't load the data.win anymore. Every time I try to open it throws this error: Lost about 5 days of progress, unfortunately, however most of it I can just do it quickly so it's not THAT big of a deal. On one side this is kinda on me for not paying attention (and for some reason not doing any backups for like an entire week) but on the other side this is a pretty dumb bug. I don't really know how Undertale Mod Tool's scripts and all that stuff work, but I think y'all should definitely make it check if all the previous frames exist before importing a new one. If there's like a super easy fix for this (that isn't making a back up) then sorry for the unnescessary report I guess. ### Reproducing steps 1. Open a data.win 2. Use the ImportGraphics.csx script. 3. Import a new sprite and have it only have a frame/index that isn't 0. (For example, import a sprite called "FunnySprite" but import it as "FunnySprite_3" instead of "FunnySprite_0") 4. Now Undertale Mod Tool shouldn't let you save and ouble clicking the sprite should instantly close the program. 5. Trying to open the data.win will just throw the same error again and again. ### Setup Details 1. I was using the "bleeding edge" version of Undertale Mod Tool. However changing to a regular release would still not let me open the game. 2. I have Windows 10. 3. https://gamejolt.com/games/frickbears3/930477 (I doubt the game itself has something to do with this problem but I'm putting it here just in case)

title: More practical features
author: Genouka
body:
### Which component should be improved?

UI/UX

### Describe your feature suggestion in more detail

I implement the following different functions in different branches to facilitate rebase: https://github.com/Genouka/UndertaleModTool/branches
 
- Allow to import resources from other datafiles. 
  ![Preview](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Genouka/UndertaleModTool/refs/heads/master/images/preview/image-3.png)
  ![Preview](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Genouka/UndertaleModTool/refs/heads/master/images/preview/image-4.png)
- Add floating information panel documentation for built-in functions, constants, and variables. 
  ![Preview](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Genouka/UndertaleModTool/refs/heads/master/images/preview/image.png)
  ![Preview](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Genouka/UndertaleModTool/refs/heads/master/images/preview/image-1.png)
- Add floating information panel documents for sprites, numbers, etc. 
  ![Preview](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Genouka/UndertaleModTool/refs/heads/master/images/preview/image-5.png)
  ![Preview](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Genouka/UndertaleModTool/refs/heads/master/images/preview/image-6.png)
- Add control options for automatic line wrapping and displaying whitespace characters to the code editor. They can also be configured with default values from the settings. 
  ![Preview](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Genouka/UndertaleModTool/refs/heads/master/images/preview/image-7.png)
  ![Preview](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Genouka/UndertaleModTool/refs/heads/master/images/preview/image-8.png)
- Support displaying recently opened files (automatically excluding invalid files). 
  ![Preview](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Genouka/UndertaleModTool/refs/heads/master/images/preview/image-9.png)

Because I am developing based on a localized branch(https://github.com/UnderminersTeam/UndertaleModTool/pull/2378), it may need some changes to rebase to upstream without merging https://github.com/UnderminersTeam/UndertaleModTool/pull/2378 .

I don't know how to do it better, so I submitted this issue firstly here.

title: Converting decimal colors to hex does not work correctly with little endian
author: Shorts404
body:
### Describe the bug

Hello, this is my first ever reported issue on GitHub, so apologies if it's not great, and I'm also not the best when it comes to highly technical stuff like big/little endian values.

I encountered this issue when editing palettes in the GIMMIKO Demo, it seems that game uses little endian for its decimal color values, because when I right click them to convert them to hex, the resulting color ends up inaccurate to the color it's replacing on the sprite.

For example, converting 10221567 in UTMT gives me 0x9BF7FF, when it should be 0xFFF79B.

### Reproducing steps

1. Right click on decimal color
2. Click the hex value color
3. Color ends up inaccurate

### Setup Details

1. UTMT v0.9.0.0 (Git: c4e5c2c)
2. Windows 11
3. GIMMIKO Demo ( https://store.steampowered.com/app/2101420/GIMMIKO/ )
